details: 30.6 pp gap between with_skill (98.6%) and old_skill (68.0%).
Evals 6 and 7 are the highest-signal new evals: they force the executor to describe Phase 6 faithfully from the skill, exposing old skill's structural absence of code-simplifier.
Eval 2 still symmetric (8/9 both) — assertion-1 (codebase discovery) fails for both because neither skill mandates discovering real codebase patterns.
Eval 5 still saturated — TDD bypass refusal is in old skill too ('always use tdd').

recommended_next_actions: Eval suite is well-calibrated: 6 of 8 evals discriminate, 2 are saturated but stable.
Eval 5 saturation is acceptable — old skill has TDD in Phase 5 already; the 'do not bypass it' enforcement is harder to test with narrative responses.
Eval 2 assertion-1 could be tightened to require explicit codebase file citations, but the overall eval still discriminates via assertion-9 in some runs.
Consider adding a third run per eval to get variance estimates before calling this stable.
